chemistry.mechanism_type: Plasmonic photocatalysis via localized surface plasmon resonance (LSPR)

photocatalyst.name: Ag/AgCl/ZnO composites
photocatalyst.role: Plasmonic photocatalyst for visible light-driven degradation

mechanistic_details.key_intermediate: Superoxide radicals (•O2-), hydroxyl radicals (HO•), and Cl0 atoms

chemistry.scope_summary: Photocatalytic degradation of organic dyes (methyl orange) and phenol under visible light irradiation using plasmonic Ag/AgCl/ZnO composites synthesized via microfluidic flow chemistry

variables_screened: molar ratio of Zn2+ to Ag+
optimization_table.key_findings: Zn2+/Ag+=17:1, total volume flow rate=1 mL/min, Qaq/Qoil=2:3, residence time=1.2 min, synthesis temperature=20°C were optimal conditions

figures.caption: Figure 3.  (A) TEM image (B) SEM image of the as-prepared Ag/AgCl/ZnO composites (C)
Figure 6.  TEM images of the as-prepared Ag/AgCl/ZnO composites at different water/oil
Figure 7.  TEM images of Ag/AgCl/ZnO composites at different total volume flow rates (A)
Figure 8.  TEM images of Ag/AgCl/ZnO composites synthesized at different temperatures (A)
Figure 9.  TEM and SEM images of Ag/AgCl/ZnO composites synthesized at different molar

translation_logic.batch_limitation: Limited data available for batch baseline conditions
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Continuous synthesis allows better control of particle morphology and size distribution
translation_logic.what_changed: Process moved from batch to continuous flow microreactor with optimized flow rates and residence times
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Photocatalyst composition (Ag/AgCl/ZnO), temperature (20°C), and visible light irradiation
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Continuous flow provides better heat and mass transfer control

figure_descriptions: TEM and SEM characterization figures showing morphology optimization
Process optimization figures showing effect of flow conditions

notes: This paper focuses on continuous synthesis of photocatalyst materials rather than batch-to-flow translation of a specific chemical reaction. The optimization data is well-documented but batch baseline conditions are limited. Most figures are characterization images rather than process schemes.
